commit.message:
iccanalyzer-lite: add external file metadata section

New EXTERNAL FILE METADATA section runs between preflight and header
validation, capturing output from available tools via popen():

  - file(1)     — magic-based type identification
  - exiftool    — structured metadata (MIME, dimensions, ICC presence)
  - tiffinfo    — TIFF IFD structure (only for TIFF-wrapped inputs)
  - identify    — ImageMagick structural analysis with warnings
  - xxd         — hex dump of first 128 bytes (ICC header)
  - sha256sum   — file hash for provenance

Tools are run with 10s timeout, filename validated against shell
metacharacters. Missing tools are silently skipped.

Also updated analyze-profile.sh to include SHA-256 and file type
in the markdown report header.

All 172 unit tests pass.

fix(agents): robust text-based tool call detection for Foundry Local

Root cause: small models (Foundry Local, Ollama) emit tool calls as plain text JSON.
Three code paths (streaming premature end fallback, tool-calling error fallback, and
the normal streaming path) had gaps causing double JSON rendering with no execution.

Changes:
- ExtractFirstToolCallJson: replace brittle single-block parser with three-strategy
  approach: bare JSON → fenced code blocks (regex) → balanced-brace scan.
  Correctly handles duplicate JSON blocks, prose around JSON, nested arguments.
- TryExecuteTextToolCallAsync: delegates extraction to ExtractFirstToolCallJson;
  no longer requires entire response to be a lone JSON object.
- Streaming premature-end fallback: call TryExecuteTextToolCallAsync on the
  non-streaming fallback response before rendering; render tool result + follow-up
  instead of re-rendering raw JSON.
- Tool-calling error fallback: same fix; also attempts text tool execution.
- All three call sites: add output.RenderToolCall() so users can see what ran.
- Add 11 unit tests covering all extraction strategies and edge cases.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(ci): fast and correct CI — GOWORK=off, skip SAM-dependent tests, 2 workers

Go job:
- GOWORK=off: go.work is gitignored (local dev only); explicitly disable
  workspace mode so CI builds cleanly from cli/worldanthem/go.mod

Playwright job:
- retries: 0 — retries multiply timeout costs and hide real failures;
  fail fast and fix rather than retry
- workers: 2 — sequential workers=1 serialised 66 tests; 2 workers
  halves wall-clock time for independent test files
- Skip game.spec.js and leaderboard.spec.js in CI via test.skip(            {                 echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_OUTPUT_MARKER___;                 PS1=;PS2=;unset HISTFILE;                 EC=0;                 echo ___BEGIN___COMMAND_DONE_MARKER___0;             }CI)
  These require SAM + LocalStack at localhost:3001 which is not
  available in CI. Without the skip each test waits its full 60-120s
  timeout before failing, turning CI into an 88+ minute hang.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use chunked buffer allocation in copy_bytes_raw/copy_bytes

Replace O(n) char-by-char allocation with O(n/64) chunked allocation.
Uses 64-byte chunks for bulk allocation followed by single-char padding
for exact-length strings. This prevents heap exhaustion on large reads
while maintaining correct string length contracts.

Previous doubling approach (O(log n)) broke string length - returned
power-of-2 sized strings instead of exact length, causing str_equals
failures in downstream code.

commit.message:
perf: optimize feTile with direct pixel operations (#149)

* perf: optimize feTile with direct pixel operations

Replace Graphics2D-based tiling with direct pixel array operations:
- Use DataBufferInt for zero-copy access to input pixels (was getRGB copy)
- Inline alpha bounds detection (eliminated alphaBounds + copyRegion methods)
- Extract tile pixels with System.arraycopy instead of Graphics2D drawImage
- Tile output with row-level System.arraycopy instead of per-tile drawImage

Benchmark improvement: gap vs JSVG reduced from ~15% to ~5%.

Closes #138

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix feTile rendering to use filter region as tile bounds

The feTile implementation was incorrectly using the non-transparent
alpha bounds as the tile region and starting the tiling from (0,0).
Per the SVG spec, the tile region should be the input's filter
primitive subregion, which defaults to the filter region. This caused
a shifted/misaligned tile pattern compared to CairoSVG, JSVG, and
EchoSVG.

Changes:
- Pass the computed filter region from applyFilter() to tile()
- Use the filter region as the tile bounds instead of alpha bounds
- Preserve correct origin offset so content stays at its position
- Fall back to alpha bounds when filter region is unavailable
- Update test to match correct SVG spec behavior (feTile on
  SourceGraphic produces no visible tiling when tile == filter region)
- Regenerate comparison PNG to match reference implementations

Fixes rendering correctness for issue #138.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: bridge SendMessage bypasses orchestration routing for multi-agent groups (#297)

## Problem

When sending a message to an orchestrator session from the **mobile
app** (via WebSocket bridge), the message went directly to instead of
through the multi-agent dispatch pipeline. The orchestrator responded as
a normal chat session — no worker dispatch, no task planning, no
synthesis.

This was the root cause of the PR Review Squad orchestrator not working
after the latest deploy.

## Root Cause

`WsBridgeServer.HandleClientMessage` called `SendPromptAsync` directly
via `Task.Run`, completely bypassing the orchestration routing that
exists in `Dashboard.razor`. The bridge had no awareness of orchestrator
sessions.

**Evidence from diagnostics:**
- Successful orchestrator sends: thread=1 (UI thread, via
Dashboard.razor → orchestration pipeline)
- Failed sends at 02:00/02:04: thread=44/65 (background, from `Task.Run`
in WsBridgeServer)
- No `[DISPATCH-ROUTE]` or `[DISPATCH]` logs — orchestration pipeline
never executed

## Fix

1. **`WsBridgeServer.cs`** — Now calls `GetOrchestratorGroupId()` before
sending. If the target session is an orchestrator, routes through
`SendToMultiAgentGroupAsync` instead of direct `SendPromptAsync`.
2. **`CopilotService.cs`** (CreateSessionAsync queue drain) — Same
pattern for newly created orchestrator sessions.
3. **`RightClickContextMenuTests.cs`** — Fix pre-existing test failure
(proximity threshold too tight after new attributes were added to the
session-item div).

## Tests

- 4 new unit tests in `MultiAgentRegressionTests.cs` (Bug #8 region)
- 3 new integration tests in `WsBridgeIntegrationTests.cs` (bridge
orchestration routing)
- Fix for 1 pre-existing test failure
- **All 2066 tests passing**

---------

commit.message:
feat: add pr-comments skill (#28)

## Summary
- Adds a new `pr-review` skill that fetches open PR review threads,
decides which comments to implement vs. decline, and closes the loop
- Uses `gh pr view` / REST API for most operations; only uses GraphQL
for thread resolution (the one thing REST can't do)
- Gives credit to reviewers via `Co-authored-by` trailers in the commit
message

## How it works
1. Fetches inline review comments via `gh api
repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/{number}/comments`
2. Uses a focused GraphQL query only to get thread node IDs + resolution
state
3. Reads current file state at each referenced location to judge
validity
4. Presents a plan for user confirmation before making any changes
5. Implements valid changes, commits with commenter credit, replies to
declined threads, resolves implemented threads via GraphQL mutation

## Test Plan
- [ ] Invoke skill on a PR with a mix of valid and invalid Copilot
review comments
- [ ] Verify valid comments are implemented and threads resolved
- [ ] Verify invalid comments receive a reply and threads remain open
- [ ] Verify `Co-authored-by` trailers appear in the commit

---

MYTUBE-368 Test: Direct access to protected route with auth failure — error message displayed instead of redirect (#366)

* MYTUBE-368 test: automate Test: Direct access to protected route with auth failure — error message displayed instead of redirect

* test(MYTUBE-368): fix auth-error tests using React fiber injection

Replace context.route() network-abort approach with page.evaluate()
fiber hook-dispatch to inject authError=true into AuthContext.

Firebase JS SDK v9+ does NOT trigger onAuthStateChanged's error
callback when network requests are blocked for fresh sessions.
It resolves immediately (~200ms) with user=null via the success
callback, so authError stays false and the authError code path
was never exercised.

Fix summary:
- Thread 1: Removed context.route() abort; inject authError=true
  via React fiber dispatch after redirect to /login completes
- Thread 2: RequireAuthComponent now instantiated and used in
  test_loading_state_resolves_after_firebase_failure
- Thread 3: Sign-in selector scoped to header a:has-text via
  SiteHeader page object (removes broad text=Sign in)
- Thread 4: Added has_auth_error_alert(), auth_error_alert_text(),
  has_sign_in_link() to SiteHeader; all header assertions use it
- Thread 5: Removed two false-positive spinner/delay tests

Result: 4/4 tests pass

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
